note: i.MX8 component health/version supervisor. MonitorService polls sub-ECU components (BLE, LPC devices, modem) for aliveness/heartbeat + firmware versions, resets via GPIO sysfs, publishes status. The BLE IComponent methods were carved out + named (ble_* below); LPC/modem bodies remain inlined under -O2 and are reconstructed as modelled IComponent overrides. Reconstructed to behaviour-oriented C in main/monitor/{src,include} (6 TUs, build clean -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic). See main/docs/monitor.md.

core.0x00109a98: main  // monitor/src/main.cpp entrypoint (already named). Builds service config (role string 'service', argv vector, config map from DAT_0014cb08), logs 'MonitorService starting' (main.cpp:0x22), constructs MonitorService (operator-new 0x100 + monitor_service_ctor), logs 'MonitorService started' (0x2f), runs it via monitor_service_run, destroys it, logs 'MonitorService shut down' (0x33). Stack-guard protected.
core.0x00120ac0: monitor_service_ctor  // MonitorService constructor (already named). Sets vtable @0x170fd0; stores component-graph build args at obj offsets 0x12..0x1a (transport@0x16, config@0x17, flag@0x18, fn-ptr@0x19, builder@0x1a); inits build-ctx via monitor_service_build_ctx_init at 0x1b; registers heartbeat std::function (manager FUN_00120480, callback target monitor_service_on_heartbeat) on the transport (vtable+0x18) with QoS 0x7d0=2000; registers topic handler keyed by 1-char token @0x14e600 into the registry, throwing std::runtime_error on duplicate-key.
core.0x001208f0: monitor_service_on_heartbeat  // Heartbeat callback for an unexpected/unregistered device. Logs WARN (level 2) 'Unexpected device is giving heartbeats %d.' at monitor_service.cpp:0x3e, device id read from heartbeat struct+2. Reached only via the registered std::function thunk (no direct callers).
core.0x00120fe0: monitor_service_run  // Public run/start. Acquires the pthread mutex at obj+0x60 (pthread_key guarded), then invokes virtual vtable+0x18 of the runner sub-object at obj+0xf8 to start the supervise loop; releases the mutex. Called from main between 'started' and 'shut down'.
core.0x00120530: monitor_service_poll_tick  // Service vtable+0x18 virtual. Polls runner 'should-run' (obj+0xf8 vtable+0x28); if true and enable flag obj+0xc0 set, runs monitor_service_supervise_components.
core.0x001204e0: monitor_service_poll_tick2  // Service vtable+0x10 virtual; identical poll-tick body to monitor_service_poll_tick (compiler-duplicated vtable slot / second tick entry).
core.0x001204c0: monitor_service_supervise_components  // Per-tick component supervise sweep. Indirect tail-jump into runner (obj+0xf8) vtable+0x10 — the dispatch jumptable that fans out to each registered component's poll/version/status handler (presence/aliveness, version reads, status publish).
core.0x00120770: monitor_service_dtor  // MonitorService destructor. Restores vtable; tears down runner sub-object at obj+0x1f (vtable+0x10 then release vtable+0x8); destroys the component registry vector obj+0x13..0x14 (0x40-byte elements via per-component dtor FUN_00147b20), frees the backing storage, then base.
core.0x00120800: monitor_service_deleting_dtor  // Deleting destructor (vtable+0x8): calls monitor_service_dtor then operator delete of the 0x100-byte object.
core.0x001215d0: monitor_service_build_ctx_init  // Trivial 4-field build-context initializer (stores transport/config/builder/fn pointers into a contiguous struct for the component-graph builder).
core.0x00120480: monitor_service_heartbeat_fn_manager  // std::function manager/clone-or-destroy thunk for the heartbeat callback (op 0=get_type_info,1=clone,2=move,3=destroy). Borderline vendor STL glue but specific to the heartbeat std::function, kept for completeness.

component_lpc.0x138bd0: gpio_export_and_configure  // Build '/sys/class/gpio/gpio<N>' path; export pin via '/sys/class/gpio/export'; set '/direction' to out/in. Errors via common_logf gpio.cpp.
component_lpc.0x139180: gpio_set_value  // Open '/sys/class/gpio/gpio<N>/value' and write 1/0 — the reset-line assert/deassert toggle.
component_lpc.0x139950: gpio_write_file  // Generic sysfs ofstream writer; throws runtime_error('Error, unable to open file ...') on failure.
component_lpc.0x134bf0: component_gpio_reset_line_ctor  // GpioResetLine/Component ctor (vtable DAT_001704e0): export+configure pin as output, store active level, drive initial reset state.
component_lpc.0x120310: component_reset_lines_ctor  // Reset-line bank ctor (vtable DAT_00170340): instantiate 4 GPIO reset lines on pins 1,0,10,11 for the sub-ECUs.
component_lpc.0x13a8d0: component_mqtt_client_ctor  // Mosquitto-connected component base ctor: vtables DAT_00170620/DAT_001706b8, ring buffers, spawns mosquitto loop pthread (FUN_0013bdd0), throws runtime_error('Mosquitto connect error: ...').
component_lpc.0x1371e0: component_format_version_string  // Format 'maj.min.patch' version string (ints joined by '.'); shared version formatter.
component_lpc.0x133030: json_get_number  // DEFERRED vendor: json value->int extractor, throws type_error('type must be number, but is <type>'). Do not reconstruct.
component_lpc.0x1304d0: main_parse_args  // getopt_long CLI parser (vVb:h = verbose/version/broker/help); prints build version. Listed for context, belongs to main module.

ble_modem.0x001221f0: monitor_print_component_status_table  // Renders the supervisor's component status table to std::cout: prints GLOBAL STATE banner + SYSTEM HEALTH line + separator rule, then iterates the IComponent* vector (at this+0x18) and emits one row per component via monitor_print_component_row. This is the primary reachable consumer of the IComponent interface that the ble/modem components implement.
ble_modem.0x00121ac0: monitor_print_component_row  // Prints one component's status row: presence marker, heartbeat id, name (vtable +0x30), type (+0x38), status (+0x40), alive flag (+0x48), a float rate/uptime with 's' suffix (+0x50), and an extra string (+0x58). Generic over any IComponent subclass including the BLE and modem components.

ble_methods._note: class 3BLE:9Component, vtable @0x171070, object 0x168 bytes, typeinfo @0x171048. Carved + named this pass (create_function on the undisassembled slots). Reconstructed in main/monitor/src/ble.c.
ble_methods.0x0012b2a0: ble_get_name  // get_name() (vt+0x30): copy of the cached component-name std::string ('ble').
ble_methods.0x0012ba50: ble_get_version  // get_version() (vt+0x40): copy of cached m_version @this+0xe0/+0xe8; published to ble/system/version_info.
ble_methods.0x0012b980: ble_get_status  // get_status() (vt+0x38): builds 0x60-byte object of three std::strings @+0x80 (reset_reason), +0xa0, +0xc0; reset_reason -> ble/system/reset_reason.
ble_methods.0x0012aa40: ble_is_alive  // is_alive() (vt+0x48): if byte @+0x60 != 0 -> false, else tail-calls the IComponent base default (0x12a990).
ble_methods.0x001268f0: ble_get_type  // get_type() (vt+0x58): RTTI/ostringstream type-name pipeline (STL glue) -> 'ble'.
ble_methods.0x001271a0: ble_publish  // poll/publish: walks the heartbeat map @+0x128 and publishes ble/heartbeat, ble/system/version_info, ble/system/reset_reason, ble/vars.
ble_methods.0x00126400: ble_dtor  // ~BleComponent(): restore vtable 0x171070, common_logf DEBUG 'destroying BLE component', destroy member strings/map, chain icomponent_base_dtor.
